findings.content: allowed-tools: Bash(git:*) Bash(printenv CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT) Bash(printenv __CFBundleIdentifier) Bash(printenv VSCODE_PID) Bash(mkdir:*) Bash(ls:*) Bash(wc:*) Bash(cd:*) Read Write Edit Grep Glob
findings.explanation: Frontmatter declares explicit, narrowly scoped permissions. Previous version used wildcard Bash(printenv:*) and Bash(env:*) — hardened to allowlist only the three IDE-detection environment variables actually consumed (CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT, __CFBundleIdentifier, VSCODE_PID). Bash(git:*) is required for diff operations against origin/develop. No exfiltration vector.

findings.content: Data flow: git diff + repo files -> mental analysis -> markdown report in <ide>/Revisiones diff/
findings.explanation: All flows are local. Reads git diff vs origin/develop, reads source files in the diff, writes a structured markdown report under .antigravity/.cursor/.vscode/docs. No network destinations, no MCP calls, no credential access.

notes: Drupal 11 diff-vs-develop code review skill following the Codex methodology (18 production-tested rules). Pure local-analysis tool: no bundled scripts, no network calls, no MCP references, no credential file access. Frontmatter hardened on 2026-04-07 to replace wildcard env access with an explicit three-variable allowlist for IDE detection. References (metodologia-codex-completa.md, plantillas-hallazgos.md) contain only Drupal best-practice guidance and code templates — no executable instructions. Reputation layer scored 50 (neutral) because the skill is first-party in this same repository; cannot independently verify.
